canto_title: The Gate of Hell and the Approach to the Acheron

scenes.summary: Dante reads the terrifying inscription upon the entrance to the eternal city of woe.
Virgil explains the difficult meaning of the words and prepares Dante for the souls they will encounter.
Virgil leads Dante into the dark depths, where the narrator is immediately moved to tears.
Dante describes the chaotic and terrifying sounds of various voices and cries echoing in the air.
Virgil explains that these souls are those who lived without honor or infamy, mixed with the neutral angels.
Virgil describes the eternal misery of those who have no hope of death and are despised by all.
Dante observes a vast, rapidly moving crowd of souls passing before him.

scenes.scene_name: The Shadow of the Great Refuser
scenes.summary: Dante recognizes the shade of a specific figure who represents the essence of the wicked sect.
Dante describes the gruesome physical suffering of the souls as they are bitten by insects.
Dante notices a crowd by a great river and asks Virgil for more information.
Dante approaches the river with downcast eyes, feeling embarrassed by his own questions.
Charon, the ferryman, appears and warns the living soul to stay away from the dead.
Virgil commands Charon to obey the divine will, causing the ferryman to fall silent.
The souls react to Charon's words with violent blasphemies against God and their own lineage.
The souls pull back to the riverbank in deep sorrow.
Using a simile of falling autumn leaves, Dante describes the unending stream of souls crossing the river.
Virgil explains why the wrathful are driven to cross the river by divine justice.
A violent tremor shakes the landscape, causing Dante to lose consciousness.
